# Introduction

We compare gaming behaviour between young people who are NEET (not in education, employment, or training; age ≤ 25) and a 1:1 propensity-score-matched sample of non-NEET players matched on age, gender, ethnicity, and country, using platform telemetry (Steam, Xbox, Nintendo) restricted to the three months following each participant's intake survey (Steam data is only available from intake onward, so a symmetric pre/post window would create unequal platform coverage).

## Top games

The table below ranks the ten most-played games (by total playtime) separately for matched NEET and non-NEET players, alongside the number and percentage of each group who played the game, minutes per player, and genre metadata from IGDB.

::: {.cell-output-display}

## Time of day

The figure below shows NEET players' playtime share relative to non-NEET players at each hour of the day. Positive values indicate hours where NEET players account for a larger share of play; negative values indicate hours where non-NEET players are relatively more active.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![NEET minus non-NEET difference in share of total playtime per hour (percentage points, local time). Bars above zero indicate hours where NEET players are proportionally more active; bars below zero indicate the reverse.](index_files/figure-docx/fig-time-of-day-1.png){#fig-time-of-day fig-align='center' width=80%}
:::
:::


## Distributions

The raincloud plots below compare the matched groups on seven variables: objective playtime and session length from telemetry, self-reported play, gaming disorder symptoms (GDT, wave 1), life satisfaction, and the perceived value of gaming for work and daily routines.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![Raincloud plots comparing matched NEET and non-NEET players on objective playtime and session length (telemetry), self-reported play, gaming disorder symptoms (GDT, wave 1), life satisfaction, and perceived gaming value. Session length is based on Xbox and Nintendo data only (Steam provides hourly aggregates, not individual sessions). Dots show individual participants; lines connect group means.](index_files/figure-docx/fig-distributions-1.png){#fig-distributions fig-align='center' width=80%}
:::
:::


## Social play modes

The figure below compares the share of playtime spent in competitive multiplayer and cooperative games between matched NEET and non-NEET players. Games are tagged as competitive if IGDB lists Multiplayer, Massively Multiplayer Online, or Battle Royale modes, and as cooperative if Co-operative or Split screen modes are listed.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![Share of playtime in competitive and cooperative game modes for matched NEET and non-NEET players. Panels are independent (a game can be both competitive and cooperative, so shares need not sum to 100%). Points show group means; horizontal lines show bootstrap 95% confidence intervals.](index_files/figure-docx/fig-social-modes-1.png){#fig-social-modes fig-align='center' width=80%}
:::
:::
